erstausgaben_1458_59_78.verification_notes: Anton Wildgans (1881–1932) was an Austrian poet and playwright who died in 1932, not 1956. The date range '1881 – 1956' in the title likely refers to the publication year 1956 being the 75th anniversary of his birth, not his death year. The title has been preserved as written. The year 1956 in the title is retained verbatim as it appears to be part of the commemorative title rather than a biographical death date. No correction applied to the title itself, but this discrepancy is noted.

Auflage, 66 S.; (5) Die Haarbeutel, 73.–78. Auflage, 66 S.; (6) Bilder zur Jobsiade (1872), 67 S., marked EA; (7) Fips, der Affe, 105.–112. Auflage, 89 S. Auflage, München Bassermann 1893, XVIII S.). api_concerned: The notation 'EA-' after the page count of the first entry is unclear — it may indicate Erstausgabe or be a truncated note. Additionally, 'Pater Filucius' appears twice in the Sammelband listing (once as XVIII S. and once as 40 S.), which is structurally confusing. The relationship between the first described work and the Sammelband is ambiguous — it is unclear whether the XVIII S. entry is a separate item or the title entry for the whole Sammelband. Human verification recommended.

briefe_120_5_15.title: Sämmtliche Romane und Novellen
briefe_120_5_15.original_entry: CERVANTES, Miguel de || Sämmtliche Romane und Novellen Aus dem Spanischen von Adelbert Keller und Friedrich Rotter. Stuttgart Metzler 1839 – 1841. Fünf Bände in zwölf Teilen . || Band I Teil 1- 2 Don Quijote 1,2 fehlt. || Band II. Teil 3. Don Quijote III. 299 S. || Teil 4. Don Quijote IV. 267 S. || Band III Don Quijote V. 328 S. || Teil 8. Novellen I. 230 S. || Band IV. Novellen II. 259 S. || Teil 10. Novellen III. 268 S. || Band V. Die Prüfungen des Persiles und der Sigismunda I. 304 S. || Teil 12. Die Prüfungen des Persiles und der Sigismunda II. 272 S. || Alle vier Bände stark beriebene Halblederbände, unterschiedlich stockfleckig.
briefe_120_5_15.verification_notes: The entry describes 'Fünf Bände in zwölf Teilen' (five volumes in twelve parts), but the volume/part numbering is inconsistent: Band I contains Teil 1–2, Band II contains Teil 3 and Teil 4, Band III contains Teil 5 (no Teil number given) and Teil 8, Band IV contains Teil 9 (no Teil number given) and Teil 10, Band V contains Teil 11 (no Teil number given) and Teil 12. Parts 5, 6, 7, 9, and 11 are not explicitly numbered in the entry, making the structure ambiguous. Additionally, the closing note says 'Alle vier Bände' (all four volumes) but the work is described as five volumes — this is an internal inconsistency in the entry. Band I (Teil 1–2, Don Quijote I and II) is noted as missing ('fehlt'). The volumes array captures what is explicitly stated; ambiguous part numbers are omitted from volume_number where not clearly given.

reisen_24_1_13.title: Ober-Italien
reisen_24_1_13.original_entry: GSELL-FELS Th. || Ober-Italien. Zweite umgearbeitete und vermehrte Auflage. Mit 10 Karten, 38 Plänen und Grundrissen, 20 Ansichten in Stahlstich, 1 Panorama und 64 Ansichten in Holschnitt. Leipzig Bibliograph. Institut 1875. 1455 S. doppelspaltig, 72 S. Annoncen-Beilage. Meyers Reisebücher. Zwei Bände von Meyers Reisen in einem Band. Solide, den Reisebüchern angepasste Bindung der Zeit ohne Aufdruck. Inhaltlich kaum Benützungsspuren erkennbar. Die 20 Stahlstiche tadellos! Annoncen-Beilage 72 S.2. Ex. 1874. OLn. Vollständig. Annoncen-Beilage 44 S. Sehr gut erhalten.
reisen_24_1_13.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Two copies are described. The first (main record) is the 1875 second edition, 1455 S. doppelspaltig with 72 S. Annoncen-Beilage, in a period binding without imprint. The second copy ('2. Ex.') is dated 1874, in OLn., with a 44 S. Annoncen-Beilage, described as very well preserved. The year difference (1875 vs. 1874) and differing Annoncen-Beilage page counts suggest these may be different printings or editions. The first copy is treated as the main record per cataloging convention. problematic_multi_volume: The entry states 'Zwei Bände von Meyers Reisen in einem Band', meaning two volumes bound as one physical book. The volume structure is ambiguous — it is unclear whether the two volumes have separate titles or page counts. The volumes array reflects the two physical copies rather than two distinct textual volumes, as no separate volume titles or page counts are given.

kinder_28_2_4.title: Onkel Toms Hütte

kinder_28_2_4.original_entry: HEROLD, Heinrich || Onkel Toms Hütte. Eine Erzählung aus dem Negerleben in den amerikanischen Sklavenstaaten. Nach Beecher-Stowe für die Jugend geschrieben von… Mit fünf Farbendruckbildern von W. Schäfer. Wesel Düms (1883). 71 S. OHLn.
kinder_28_2_4.verification_notes: This is a youth adaptation of Harriet Beecher Stowe's 'Uncle Tom's Cabin', rewritten by Heinrich Herold — not a translation per se, but a retelling ('für die Jugend geschrieben'). The original work is by Beecher Stowe, and Herold is the adapter. The entry lists Herold as the author, which is consistent with the cataloging convention for adaptations. is_translation is set to false since this is an adaptation rather than a direct translation. The subtitle note 'Nach Beecher-Stowe' clarifies the source. Flagged for human review to confirm authorship attribution is handled correctly.

kinder_39_2_4.verification_notes: Elisha Kent Kane was an American Arctic explorer; this appears to be a German biographical/adventure compilation about him, edited by J.G. Kutzner. The subtitle 'Jugend, Schicksale, Reisen und Entdeckungen von…' with an ellipsis suggests Kane is the subject rather than the author, and the work was compiled/edited by Kutzner. Kane may be better classified as the subject rather than the author. Human verification recommended to determine correct authorship attribution.

kinder_45_2_4.verification_notes: No publication year is given in the entry. The publisher 'Union Deutsche Verlagsgesellschaft' was active in Stuttgart roughly from the 1880s to the early 20th century, but no specific year can be determined from the entry alone. Additionally, 'Martin Grief' is likely a pseudonym for Friedrich Hermann Frey (1839–1911), a German poet — noted for awareness but not corrected. No editor is explicitly named; the contributors are listed as providing 'Beiträge' (contributions).

ethnologie_96_4_13.verification_notes: The author's given name is listed as 'Levi', but Claude Lévi-Strauss's first name was 'Claude', not 'Levi'. This may be a cataloging error (confusing the surname with the given name), but since the entry clearly states 'Levi' and no correction is unambiguous without human review, the name has been left as written. Additionally, the standard form of the name should include diacritics: 'Lévi-Strauss'. The work 'Das wilde Denken' (La Pensée sauvage, 1962) is indeed by Claude Lévi-Strauss and is a translation from French; no translator is named in the entry.

reisen_303_13_13.verification_notes: The name 'WAGNER, Richard' most commonly refers to the famous composer (1813–1883). However, there is also a contemporary Romanian-German author named Richard Wagner (born 1952) who writes travel literature and prose, and who published a book titled 'Reise in das Innere des Balkan' with Aufbau-Verlag in 2003. The entry almost certainly refers to this latter Richard Wagner, not the composer. Human verification is recommended to confirm the correct person.

reisen_310_13_13.verification_notes: The entry states 'In drei Bänden' (in three volumes) at the end, but the content describes four numbered volumes (Erster, Zweiter, Dritter, Vierter Band), making the volume count contradictory. The entry also notes that 'Dritter Band erste Abtheilung fehlt' (Third volume, first part is missing), which may explain the discrepancy — the physical set as held may consist of three bound volumes despite the work being published in four numbered volumes. Each volume has a different edition number (15th, 16th, 15th, 5th), indicating multiple editions across volumes; this is flagged under multiple_editions. The missing first Abtheilung of Band 3 is noted in the volume entry for Band 3. total_volumes is set to 4 per the textual content, not the 'In drei Bänden' note.

illustrierte_222_9_14.verification_notes: The entry contains two distinct bibliographic records concatenated together: (1) 'Lieder des Mirza-Schaffy' (Berlin, Schenck, 1922, 247 S.) with a prologue by Friedrich von Bodenstedt — parsed as the main record; (2) HOFFMANN, E.T.A., 'Doge und Dogaressa' with original lithographs by Ernst Huber (Wien, Knepler, 147 S.) — a completely separate work by a different author, not a second edition of the first. The multiple_editions flag is set because two separate entries appear to have been merged. The api_concerned flag is set because the second entry ('Doge und Dogaressa') is a separate work that cannot be parsed into this record without losing data; it requires its own record. The year of the Knepler edition of 'Doge und Dogaressa' is missing. 'Lieder des Mirza-Schaffy' is a collection of poems attributed to the fictional Mirza-Schaffy, popularized by Friedrich von Bodenstedt — no single author is listed, which is consistent with the entry.

bibliophile_7_1_10.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes two copies/editions of the same work. The first is the complete three-part volume (München, Bremer Presse und Berlin Rowohlt, 1923–1930, 522 S., OLn., tadellose Erhaltung). The second is a separate copy of the 'Fegefeuer' (Purgatorio) section only (S. 164–312, OPbd. mit Deckel- und Rückenschild, neuwertig), described as 'wie oben' (same publisher/year). The main record reflects the first-described complete volume. api_concerned: The publication year range '1923 bis 1930' spans multiple years; the three parts of Borchardt's Dante translation were indeed published separately over this period by Bremer Presse. The dual publisher listing 'München Bremer Presse und Berlin Rowohlt' is unusual and may reflect different publishers for different parts. Human verification of the exact publication details is recommended. Rudolf Borchardt is both the translator and the named author in the title.

The main record has been assigned to the Juvenal work as first described. The Persius volume is noted in condition. No publisher is given for the Juvenal volume. The format 'OHLdr.' (Originalhalbleder) applies to the combined binding. The second work is a separate publication and not a translation entry per se — its translator/editor status is ambiguous. Human verification recommended to determine whether two separate records should be created.

It is unclear whether the page counts correspond to Bands 1, 3, and possibly another band, or whether the numbering refers to 'Teile' (parts) rather than physical volumes. The volumes array has been populated conservatively: Band 1 (XVI+260 S.), and the two remaining counts assigned to Bands 3 and 4 tentatively, but this assignment is uncertain. Additionally, the work is Alexander von Humboldt's 'Relation historique du Voyage aux régions équinoxiales du Nouveau Continent', originally in French; the author has been inferred from context ('A. v. erstausgaben_716_29_78.verification_notes: Place and publisher order corrected: the entry reads 'Suhrkamp Berlin', but standard cataloging convention is place first, then publisher. Corrected to place_of_publication: 'Berlin', publisher: 'Suhrkamp'. Additionally, api_concerned: Suhrkamp was founded in Frankfurt am Main, not Berlin; in 1946 it was still part of the Suhrkamp Verlag operating under the umbrella of S. Fischer in Berlin/Frankfurt. The place 'Berlin' may be correct for this early postwar edition, but the combination warrants human verification. Also, 'Der Europäer' is not a widely known standalone work by Hermann Hesse — this may refer to a short prose piece or pamphlet; the title and page count (71 S.) are plausible for a small Suhrkamp publication of that era, but verification is recommended.

antike_149_6_9.title: Sämtliche Schriften
antike_149_6_9.original_entry: XENOPHONS || Sämtliche Schriften. Aus dem Griechischen neu übersetzt von August Christian Borhek. || Erster Theil, welcher die Kyropädie mit den vornehmsten Abhandlungen über selbige enthält. Dazu Anhang ,welcher einige der besten Erläuterungsschriften der Kyropädie enthält. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette. XVI, 535 S. || Zweyter Theil, welcher den Feldzug des Prinzen Kyros, mit den besten Erläuterungsschriften desselben enthält. Dazu Thomas Hutchinsons Abhandlung , vom Verfasser des Feldzugs des Prinzen Kyros, und vom Zuge der Griechen. Im Auszuge. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette VI, 413 S. || Dritter Theil, welcher die griechische Geschichte enthält und einer Widmung des Übersetzers Borheck von 1789. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette (XVIII), 509 S || Vierter Theil. Erster Anhang zur griechischen Geschichte Xenophons, oder Einleitung in dieselbe. Dazu Xenophons Lobschrift auf den König Agesilaus, übersetzt von Konrad Borheck. Weiters Xenophons Staatsverfassung der Lakedämonier. Übersestzt von Konrad Borheck. Weiters Xenophon über die Einkünfte, Xenophons Hiero und Xenophons Briefe übersetzt von Konrad Borheck. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette. 464 S. || Wien und Prag bey Franz Haas 1801. Original grüne Pappbände mit Rückentitel, stark beansprucht. Buchblock, Text und Kupfer tadellos erhalten. Der fünfte Band fehlt. Seltenes Werk. Band 1 – 4 von 5
antike_149_6_9.verification_notes: 1) CORRECTED: The entry heading reads 'XENOPHONS' (genitive form); normalized to 'XENOPHON' as the author name. 2) CORRECTED: The translator's name is spelled inconsistently throughout the entry as both 'Borhek' (in the main title line) and 'Borheck' (in volumes 3 and 4). The form 'Borheck' appears more frequently and is used for the named co-translator Konrad Borheck; however, the correct historical spelling of the main translator is 'Borheck' — corrected_by_api set true for this normalization, though api_concerned is also set because both spellings appear in the original and the correct form cannot be verified with certainty. 3) PROBLEMATIC MULTI-VOLUME: The entry explicitly states 'Band 1–4 von 5' and notes 'Der fünfte Band fehlt.' Volume 5 is absent and its content, page count, and title are unknown; it has been omitted from the volumes array. 4) API_CONCERNED: Konrad Borheck is listed as a separate translator for portions of volume 4 (Lobschrift auf Agesilaus, Staatsverfassung der Lakedämonier, Hiero, Briefe, Einkünfte), distinct from the main translator August Christian Borhek/Borheck. The relationship between the two translators (possibly father and son or relatives) cannot be confirmed without further research. 5) TYPO NOTED: 'Übersestzt' in the original entry is an obvious typo for 'Übersetzt'; not corrected in the original_entry field but noted here.

The 'title' field has been used descriptively to capture the nature of the object.

autographen_34_2_2.verification_notes: This entry describes a multi-item autograph/memorabilia convolute rather than a standard bibliographic item. It contains: (1) a handwritten 42-line letter by Marianne Ziehrer, actress and wife of the composer; (2) a film programme from Ideal-Film-Gesellschaft for 'Carl Michael Ziehrer – Der letzte Walzerkönig' with song texts on four pages; (3) several signed photographs of C.M. and Marianne Ziehrer. The entry does not correspond to a single published work and cannot be fully represented in standard bibliographic fields. Human review is recommended to determine appropriate cataloging treatment.

oesterreich_153_7_13.verification_notes: The entry notes the text is by Franz Ferdinand von Schrötter, listed here as contributor. The copper plates are noted as sourced from 'Wening: Historico-Topographica Descriptio 1701-1726' (Michael Wening's well-known Bavarian topographical work). The authorship attribution to Schrötter is given parenthetically and may warrant verification; no author is formally listed on the title page as described.

kunstbuecher_7_1_38.title: Collection de cent silhouettes des personnes illustres et celebres dessinees d`apres les originaux
kunstbuecher_7_1_38.original_entry: ANTHING, (Johann Friedrich) || Collection de cent silhouettes des personnes illustres et celebres dessinees d`apres les originaux. Gotha 1791. Neudruck Weimar Gesellschaft der Bibliophilen 1913. 100 lose Blätter mit ganzseitigen Silhouetten in Lichtdruck. Dazu Broschüre 'Johann Friedrich Anthing. Eine Skizze von Carl Schüddekopf.' In einer bunten Originalkassette. Sehr gut erhalten.
kunstbuecher_7_1_38.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes the original Gotha 1791 edition and a Neudruck (Weimar, Gesellschaft der Bibliophilen, 1913). Per convention, the first-described edition (Gotha 1791) would normally be the main record, but the physical description (100 loose sheets in Lichtdruck, accompanying Broschüre by Carl Schüddekopf, Originalkassette) clearly belongs to the 1913 Neudruck, which is the copy actually held. The 1913 Neudruck has therefore been used as the main record. The original 1791 Gotha edition is noted here as the source publication. The accompanying Broschüre 'Johann Friedrich Anthing. Eine Skizze von Carl Schüddekopf' is included with the copy.

sonstiges_71_3_4.title: SCHATZKAMMER MECHANISCHER KÜNSTE
sonstiges_71_3_4.subtitle: Des hoch- und weitberühmeten Capitains Herrn Augustini de Ramellis, de Masanzana…durch Henning Großen den Jüngern im Jahr MDCXX. Nachdruck aus dem Jahre 1620
sonstiges_71_3_4.original_entry: SCHATZKAMMER MECHANISCHER KÜNSTE || Des hoch- und weitberühmeten Capitains Herrn Augustini de Ramellis, de Masanzana…durch Henning Großen den Jüngern im Jahr MDCXX. Nachdruck aus dem Jahre 1620. Hannover Vinzentz Verlag 1976. 319 S. Mit zahlr. Abb. OPbd.
sonstiges_71_3_4.verification_notes: The entry mentions 'durch Henning Großen den Jüngern' which refers to the original 1620 German publisher/translator Henning Grosse the Younger. The author Agostino Ramelli (c.1531–c.1600) wrote 'Le diverse et artificiose machine' (1588) in Italian/French; this is a German reprint of the 1620 edition. The author is not explicitly named in the entry — 'Augustini de Ramellis' in the subtitle identifies him. Author has been inferred from world knowledge; flagged for verification. Publisher name 'Vinzentz Verlag' in Hannover is noted but not widely documented for this title.

philosophie_577_24_24.verification_notes: The entry describes the 1677 posthumous collected works of Spinoza (Opera posthuma), containing multiple works: Praefatio (pp. 270–284), Ethica ordine geometrico demonstrata (pp. 288–518), and Epistolae with Responsiones (pp. 522–664). The page ranges suggest this is a specific copy of the original 1677 edition, but no place of publication or publisher is given. The original 1677 edition was published in Amsterdam. The page numbering starting at 270 suggests this may be a partial copy or bound extract rather than a complete volume — human verification is recommended to confirm the physical nature of the copy.

briefe_36_2_15.verification_notes: The entry describes a two-part/two-volume work but the structure is ambiguous: the first part has 'XXXII, 159 SS.' and the second part ('II. Theil') has '138, 34 gez. S.' — it is unclear whether these are two physical volumes or two parts within one volume. Volume 1 page count is given as XXXII + 159 pages but no numeric integer can be cleanly assigned. Parsed as two volumes with page details in notes. Additionally, Ewald Christian von Kleist (1715–1759) was a German poet and friend of Lessing; the parenthetical '(Freund Lessings)' confirms this identification. However, a 'Sämtliche Werke' published in Wien by Bauer in 1816 should be verifiable — the cataloger's note is consistent with known biographical facts. briefe_361_15_15.verification_notes: The volume structure is complex and partially ambiguous. The entry lists 16 line-break-separated content blocks but the mapping of content blocks to volume numbers is not explicitly stated — the assignment here is inferred from the sequence. Band XV is explicitly identified as 'C. M. Wielands Leben von J. G. Gruber', and Band XVI as 'Comische Erzählungen Combabus. Der verklagte Amor.' The phrase 'als Band XVI doppelt' is unclear — it may mean a duplicate copy of Band XVI exists, or that Band XVI is counted twice in the set. The publisher name is not explicitly stated in the entry (only the place 'Hamburg' and year 1984 are given); the publisher is likely 'Hamburger Stiftung zur Förderung von Wissenschaft und Kultur' or a commercial imprint, but cannot be confirmed from the entry alone. The total page count of 'ca. 6400 Seiten' is approximate. The note 'Nr. 5692' suggests a numbered copy of Band XVI specifically.

buddhismus_1_1_3.verification_notes: The editor/commentator name 'S. Radhkrishnan' likely refers to Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, the Indian philosopher and statesman, whose name is commonly spelled 'Radhakrishnan' (with an 'a' after 'Radh'). The entry spells it 'Radhkrishnan', which may be a typo. However, since this could also be a legitimate abbreviated or variant spelling, the name has not been corrected and api_concerned is set. Additionally, no publication year is given. The entry mentions 'Lizenz Holle Verlag', indicating this is a licensed edition from Holle Verlag, published by the Buchgemeinschaft.

The translation chain is complex: Burton and Arbuthnot produced an English translation from Sanskrit; Kolb and Weltmann then translated that into German. Both pairs are listed as contributors accordingly. Friedrich Ziehlke is listed as editor/reviser who compared against the original Sanskrit text and wrote the foreword.

autographen_10_1_2.verification_notes: This entry describes a collection of 9 autograph letters (handwritten and typewritten) by Lou Eisler, addressed to Charlotte Eisler and/or Georg Eisler, written between 1939 and 1946 from various locations (Los Angeles, New York, Mexico, California). The entry includes biographical context: Lou Eisler (1906–1998), translator and publicist, was the wife of Hanns Eisler and later of Ernst Fischer. The entry is an autograph/manuscript item rather than a conventional bibliographic record; standard bibliographic fields such as publisher, year, and pages are not applicable. api_concerned is set because 'Lou Eisler' as wife of Hanns Eisler is not a widely documented figure under that name — she may be better known as 'Louise Eisler' or under another name variant; human verification of the identity is recommended.

autographen_14_1_2.title: Hs. Brief an Alice Eisler
autographen_14_1_2.original_entry: FRANCK, Martine || Fotografin, Mitglied von Magnum 1938-2012. Hs. Brief an Alice Eisler, 8 Zeilen aus Paris 07/02 auf einem Foto der Künstlerin. || Ansichtskarte mit hs. Adresse und 10 zeiligem Text der Fotografin an Alice Eisler vom 6/12/17.
autographen_14_1_2.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes two distinct autograph items — (1) a handwritten letter of 8 lines from Paris, dated 07/02, on a photograph of the artist, addressed to Alice Eisler; and (2) a postcard with handwritten address and 10-line text addressed to Alice Eisler, dated 6/12/17. These are treated as two separate physical objects; the first is used as the main record. api_concerned: Martine Franck (1938–2012) was indeed a Belgian-French photographer and Magnum member, consistent with the entry. However, the date '6/12/17' on the second item postdates her death in 2012, which is contradictory. This may be a cataloging error (possibly the year 2007 or another date) and requires human verification. Additionally, the dates '07/02' and '6/12/17' are ambiguous in format (day/month/year vs. month/year).

The Viertes Bändchen is mentioned in the entry but the series note only references 17., 18. and 19. Band (i.e., Bändchen 1, 2, and 3), suggesting the Viertes Bändchen may belong to a different series volume or the series note is incomplete. The entry says 'alle drei Bände gut erhalten' confirming three physical items, yet four Bändchen are described. Volume structure is therefore ambiguous. Additionally, place_of_publication and publisher cannot be assigned unambiguously at the top level since different Bändchen were published in München and Leipzig.

Roman in sechs Büchern. 862 S. || STORM, Theodor: Werke in einem Band. 784 S. || FONTANE, Theodor: Werke in vier Bänden. 890, 899, 808, 877 SS. || KELLER, Gottfried: Werke in zwei Bänden. 768, 956 SS. || MEYER, Conrad Ferdinand: Werke in einem Band. 808 SS. || RAABE, Wilhelm: Werke in einem Band. 930 S. || NIETZSCHE, Friedrich: Werke in drei Bänden. 1279, 1275, 1476 SS. || Vier Bände Goethe (auf den Positionen 9 – 12) fehlen. Insgesamt sind es 56 Bände. Jeder Band ist mit einem ausführlichen Kommentar, Namensregistern, Registern der Gedichtanfänge- und –Überschriften ausgestattet. Alle Bände dunkelrote Originalleinenbände mit schwarzen Rückenschildern und Goldschmuck, alle mit Lesebändchen. Sämtliche Bände gleichförmig unbenutzt und makellos erhalten. || Statt der fehlenden vier Goethe-Bänden: || GOETHE, Johann Wolfgang von || Poetische Werke in zehn Bänden. Gesamtausgabe Stuttgart Weltbild Bücherdienst || Gedichte. 1383 S. || West-Östlicher Divan. Epen Maximen und Reflexionen. 913 S. || Lustspiele. Singspiele. Satiren. Dramatische Zeit- und Gelegenheitsdichtungen. 1406 S. || Frühe Dramen. Bruchstücke. Übersetzungen und Bearbeitungen. 1258 S. || Die großen Dramen. 1070 S. || Erzählende Dichtungen. Erster Teil. 1229 S. || Erzählende Dichtungen Zweiter Teil. 1310 S. || Autobiographische Schriften Erster Teil. 1533 S. || Autobiographische Schriften Zweiter Teil. 919 S. || Autobiographische Schriften Dritter Teil. Mit einem Gesamtregister zu den Bänden 8,9,10. || Alle zehn Bände in gleicher Ausstattung wie die oben beschriebenen, sodass die Bibliothek außen eine attraktive Einheit bildet.
briefe_1_1_15.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: 'HERDER, Johann Gottried' corrected to 'Johann Gottfried' (clear typo). 'SCHOPENHAUER, Arthur von' — Schopenhauer did not use the noble particle 'von'; flagged under api_concerned but name left as written in entry. multiple_editions: The set as described is the Harenberg Kommunikation 1982 Sonderausgabe (56 volumes, missing 4 Goethe volumes); the missing Goethe volumes are supplemented by a separate 10-volume Goethe Gesamtausgabe published by Weltbild Bücherdienst, Stuttgart (year not stated). These are two distinct publications combined into one physical collection. The Harenberg edition is treated as the main record; the Weltbild Goethe volumes are noted as supplementary. problematic_multi_volume: The edition was originally planned as 60 volumes; 4 Goethe volumes (positions 9–12) are absent from the Harenberg set, making the actual Harenberg count 56. The supplementary Weltbild Goethe set has 10 volumes, making the combined total 66 volumes, but these are not uniform editions. Volume numbers within each author's sub-set are not individually numbered in the entry, making precise volume-number assignment impossible. The Weltbild Goethe Bd. 10 (Autobiographische Schriften Dritter Teil) has no page count given. api_concerned: 'SCHOPENHAUER, Arthur von' — Schopenhauer is not known to have used 'von'; this may be a cataloging error. Also, the Bibliothek Deutscher Klassiker is typically associated with Hanser Verlag (copyright 1977 noted in entry) and the Harenberg Sonderausgabe 1982 is a known reprint; the editor Karl Schlechta is primarily known for his Nietzsche edition, not as general editor of this entire series — this warrants verification.

briefe_4_1_15.verification_notes: This entry catalogues a large multi-series collected works of Brecht across five distinct series (Stücke, Gedichte, Schriften zur Literatur und Kunst, Prosa, Schriften zum Theater), totalling 35 volumes. The entry header states 'STÜCKE Band I–X' but then lists 13 Stücke volumes; the Gedichte section is announced as 'Band I–IX' but lists 9 volumes (some with duplicate date ranges, e.g. two volumes of 'Gedichte 1934–1941' and two volumes of 'Nachträge'). The 'V.' before 'Me-Ti' in the Prosa section is unclear — possibly a volume number or a stray character. Volume numbering across series has been assigned sequentially (1–35) for structural clarity, but the original entry does not provide a unified numbering across all series. Years are missing for several Stücke aus dem Exil volumes (Bände 7–10). The entry is flagged as problematic_multi_volume due to these ambiguities. api_concerned is set because the two 'Gedichte 1934–1941' volumes and two 'Nachträge' volumes with different page counts may represent distinct volumes or duplicate entries requiring verification.

Neue Folge) plus ein Reclam-Bändchen. Die genaue Seitenzahl für Band 1 als Ganzes ist nicht eindeutig angebbar (IX+267 + IV+218 + 102 S.). Band 2 (Zeltleben in Sibirien) ist klar mit VII, 355 S. beschrieben.

biographie_56_3_27.title: Mein Leben. Erinnerungen aus Österreichs großer Welt
biographie_56_3_27.original_entry: THÜRHEIM, Gräfin Lulu || Mein Leben. Erinnerungen aus Österreichs großer Welt. Herausgegeben von Rene van Rhyn. München Georg Müller. || 1788 – 1819. 1923. XVI, 390 S. Mit dreiunddreißig Bildbeigaben. || 1788 – 1819. 1923. 372 S. Mit 40 Bildbeigaben. || 1819 – 1852. 1914. XII, 382 S. Mit sechsunddreißig Bildbeigaben. || 1819 – 1852. 1914. XII, 326 S. Mit sechsunddreißig Bildbeigaben. || Vier Originalleinenbände mit Lesebändchen, tadellose Erhaltung
biographie_56_3_27.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry lists four volumes but appears to describe two different sets of two volumes each — one set published 1923 (covering 1788–1819) and one set published 1914 (covering 1819–1852). Within each period, two entries with differing page counts and illustration counts are given, suggesting either two copies or two editions of each volume. It is unclear whether this is a 2-volume work with duplicate copies, or a 4-volume set. The entry concludes with 'Vier Originalleinenbände', suggesting four physical volumes are present. problematic_multi_volume: The volume numbering is ambiguous — volumes are identified only by date ranges, not by explicit volume numbers. The two entries for '1788–1819' and the two for '1819–1852' may represent different copies or different editions of the same volumes. api_concerned: The work by Gräfin Lulu Thürheim is known as a 4-volume memoir published by Georg Müller, München, but the discrepancy in page counts and illustration counts between the two entries for each period cannot be resolved without physical inspection. The editor's name 'Rene van Rhyn' may be a variant spelling of René van Rhyn — flagged for verification.

erstausgaben_1106_45_78.verification_notes: This entry is a Konvolut (collection) of 17 separate secondary literature items on Karl Kraus, parsed as individual volumes. Corrections applied: (1) 'es Erscheinens' → 'des Erscheinens' (obvious typo); (2) 'Mrchthilde' → 'Mechthilde' (clear typo for Mechthilde Lichnowsky); (3) 'Mi einem' → 'Mit einem' (obvious typo); (4) 'OKartt' → 'OKart' (duplicate letter); (5) 'Gebrutstag' → 'Geburtstag' (obvious typo); (6) 'Frühhjahr' → 'Frühjahr' (duplicate letter). API concern: The entry lists 'KRAUS, Karl' as the author/heading, but Karl Kraus is the subject of all items, not their author. The heading appears to be a subject/thematic grouping rather than an authorship attribution. Additionally, 'ERNST KRENEK' appears as a heading without surname-first format, suggesting it may be Ernst Krenek (composer, 1900–1991); this is plausible given the 1937 date and Vienna context. The Rychner entry title reads 'Jahrestag es Erscheinens' which is clearly 'des Erscheinens'. The 'Studien über Karl Kraus' and 'Rundfrage über Karl Kraus' items have no named author/editor in the entry.

erstausgaben_1107_45_78.verification_notes: Multiple editions flag: Several volumes are listed with different edition designations (Drittes und viertes Tausend, 2. Auflage, EA), making it impossible to assign a single edition to the top-level record. The first-described volume (Band I, Drittes und viertes Tausend 1925) is used as the main record's edition and pages. api_concerned: Volumes II, VII, and VIII are absent from the entry — it is unclear whether these are not owned or simply not listed; human verification recommended. problematic_multi_volume: The volume sequence is non-consecutive (I, III, IV, V, VI, IX), with volumes II, VII, VIII missing. Only the clearly described volumes have been entered in the volumes array.

erstausgaben_1108_45_78.title: Die letzten Tage der Menschheit
erstausgaben_1108_45_78.subtitle: Tragödie in fünf Akten mit Vorspiel und Epilog
erstausgaben_1108_45_78.original_entry: KRAUS, Karl || Die letzten Tage der Menschheit. Tragödie in fünf Akten mit Vorspiel und Epilog von … Sonderheft der Fackel. Wien Verlag 'Die Fackel' . || Vorspiel und I. Akt 1919. 122 S. || II. und III. Akt. Mit einem Frontispiz. 126-280 S. || IV. und V. Akt. Mit einem Frontispiz 286-639 S. || Epilog zur Tragödie Die letzten Tage der Meschheit. Geschrieben im Juli 1917 zu Thierfeld (Glarus). Wien Verlag 'Die Fackel' Wien. November 1918. mit einem Frontispiz 48 S. || Alle vier Teile OBrosch. Band I Rücken defekt, Band II,III sehr gut erhalten, Band IV,V. nur die letzten 10 Blätter lose, sehr gut erhalten, Epilog Rücken Defekt, Titelblatt etwas abgegriffen. || Zusammen
erstausgaben_1108_45_78.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: The Epilog title in the entry reads 'Die letzten Tage der Meschheit' (missing 'n'), corrected to 'Die letzten Tage der Menschheit'. api_concerned: The entry describes the work as a 'Sonderheft der Fackel' published in parts; the first complete book edition was published by Verlag 'Die Fackel' in 1922. The parts described here (1919 and November 1918 for the Epilog) correspond to the original Fackel Sonderhefte, not the collected book edition. The year 1919 is given only for the first part; no publication years are stated for parts II/III and IV/V — these may have appeared in 1919 or later. Human verification of the exact publication dates for each part is recommended. problematic_multi_volume: The entry lists four physical items (Vorspiel+I, II+III, IV+V, Epilog) but the work has five acts plus Vorspiel and Epilog. Page ranges are given as continuous (126–280, 286–639) suggesting a single pagination sequence across parts, but part I starts at p. 1 and part II at p. 126 (gap: pp. 123–125 unaccounted). The Epilog has its own pagination (48 S.). Page counts for volumes 2 and 3 are calculated from the stated ranges and may not reflect actual printed page counts.

erstausgaben_1111_45_78.title: Das Notwendige und das Überflüssige

erstausgaben_1111_45_78.original_entry: KRAUS, Karl || Das Notwendige und das Überflüssige (Nach 'Die beiden Nachtwandler'). Posse mit Gesang in zwei Akten von Johann Nestroy. Bearbeitet von Karl Kraus. Mit einer Notenbeilage. Wien Lanyi 1920. 53, 11 S. 2 OPbde. Ränder etwas beschädigt, sonst sehr gut erhalten.
erstausgaben_1111_45_78.verification_notes: The entry lists '2 OPbde.' which could mean 2 copies in Originalpappband binding, or a 2-volume set. Given the page count '53, 11 S.' (likely 53 + 11 pages = 64 total), a 2-volume interpretation seems unlikely for such a short work; 2 copies is the more probable reading. Pages summed to 64. Additionally, Karl Kraus is listed as author/editor (he adapted the Nestroy work), so his role is ambiguous between author and editor/adapter — listed as author per entry header. Nestroy listed as contributor as the original playwright.

russland_4_1_17.verification_notes: The volume structure is complex and partially ambiguous. Band 1 appears to consist of two sub-volumes (Band 1.1 with X, 543 S. and Band 1.2 'Forschungen, Briefe und Dokumente' with 184 S.). Band 2 similarly has two sub-volumes (Erste Abteilung: VII, 615 S. and Zweite Abteilung: 376 S.). The notation 'Band 2. 1.2. Band 2.' is unclear and may indicate sub-volume numbering. Additionally, Band 2 was published by a different publisher (Verlag Siegfried Cronbach, Berlin, 1893), making this a multiple_editions / split-publisher situation. The main record uses the first-described publisher (Norddeutsches Verlags-Institut, 1891). The second publisher and year (Siegfried Cronbach, 1893) apply to Band 2 and are noted in the volumes array.
